description: Compare fund investment, co-investment, and direct investment methods for accessing alternatives, contrasting diversification, fees, control, and required expertise

fund_investment.description: Investor commits capital to a pooled vehicle run by a GP
fund_investment.pros: Diversification, access to specialised managers, due diligence outsourced
fund_investment.cons: Double fee drag (FoF), limited control, lock-ups, J-curve

co_investment.description: Invest alongside fund in a specific deal, usually fee-reduced
co_investment.pros: Lower fees, selective exposure, closer to manager
co_investment.cons: Concentration risk, requires in-house deal analysis

direct_investment.description: Investor purchases the asset or company directly
direct_investment.pros: Full control, no intermediary fees, bespoke structuring

select_access_method.description: Pick alt access method consistent with investor capability

errors.message: method must be fund, co_investment, or direct

anti_patterns.rule: Do not direct-invest without internal sourcing and due diligence capacity
anti_patterns.why: Adverse selection is severe; deals shown to retail investors tend to be what funds declined

anti_patterns.rule: Do not over-allocate to fund-of-funds for alt exposure
anti_patterns.why: Double layer of fees can consume most of the illiquidity premium

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore commitment vs. funded capital difference
anti_patterns.why: Uncalled commitments create liquidity risk at capital calls; over-commitment strategies have blown up in stress
